report_name: DragonForce Ransomware Group Claims Breach of Top Indian University BITS Pilani

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that claimed responsibility for a data breach at the Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani (BITS Pilani) on June 23, 2026. The group operates a dark web leak site and uses double extortion tactics.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: The DragonForce group claimed to have stolen data from the university's systems as part of a double extortion tactic.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: As a ransomware group, DragonForce likely encrypted the university's systems to disrupt operations and demand a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely exfiltrated large volumes of sensitive data to their own servers before deploying ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: A common precursor to ransomware deployment is the deletion of volume shadow copies using tools like vssadmin.exe to prevent easy system recovery.

attack_patterns.name: Remote System Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware operators often use Active Directory discovery tools like adfind.exe for network reconnaissance to map out the network and identify valuable data stores.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware precursor activity often includes disabling security tools to evade detection during the attack.

malware.name: DragonForce Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware used by the DragonForce group to encrypt victim systems and exfiltrate data for extortion.

identities.name: Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani
identities.description: A prominent private university in India targeted by the DragonForce ransomware group in a data breach incident claimed on June 23, 2026.

infrastructure.name: DragonForce Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web leak site used by the DragonForce group to publish stolen data from victims who do not pay the ransom.

infrastructure.name: DragonForce Exfiltration Server
infrastructure.description: Servers controlled by the DragonForce group used to store exfiltrated data from victim networks.

indicators.name: Active Directory Discovery with AdFind
indicators.pattern:
title: Active Directory Discovery with AdFind
id: 4f5b6c7d-8e9f-4a1b-2c3d-4e5f6a7b8c9d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of AdFind, a common tool used by ransomware actors for Active Directory reconnaissance.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-23
tags:
  - attack.t1018
  - attack.discovery
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\adfind.exe'
    OriginalFileName: 'AdFind.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use by system administrators, though uncommon.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the execution of adfind.exe, a tool commonly used for Active Directory reconnaissance prior to ransomware deployment.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware tactic to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-23
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, but should be investigated.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects attempts to delete volume shadow copies using vssadmin.exe, a technique used by ransomware to prevent system recovery.

relationships.target_name: Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani

relationships.source_name: Birla Institute of Technology and Science, Pilani
